pages.title: Introduction
pages.intro: ARAH Harness is a repo-first harness for governing, coordinating and evolving human-agent software engineering.

blocks.text: What it installs

The harness installs and maintains an operational layer inside your repository: agent manifests, skills, domains, specialists, path-based choreography, autonomy policies, executable gates, a signal bus, an audit ledger, an agent graph, discovery, evolution and regeneration.
The core idea

Most AI tooling starts from the agent: a prompt grants authority, tools execute, code changes. ARAH inverts this. The repository identifies context, selects agents, defines limits, applies procedures, validates results and records evidence. The repository governs the agent.

items: Repo-first: contracts are versioned files, not platform settings
Model agnostic: works with Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, Codex, local models
Human in command: merge authority is always human

pages.title: Requirements
pages.intro: What you need before installing the harness.

items: Windows with PowerShell 5.1+ (or PowerShell 7+ on any OS)
Optional: GitHub CLI (gh) for PR automation
Optional: an AI environment — Claude Code, Cursor or Copilot

blocks.text: Notes
The CLI is PowerShell-based. On macOS and Linux, install PowerShell 7 (pwsh) and substitute pwsh for powershell in the commands.

pages.title: Installation
pages.intro: Clone the harness once, then install it into any consumer repository.

blocks.text: What install creates

items: AGENTS.md — the entry contract for AI environments

pages.title: First Run
pages.intro: Validate the installation and let the harness observe your repository.

blocks.text: doctor checks kernel integrity, config validity, schema conformance and directory structure. Fix anything it reports before proceeding.
discover inspects stack, frameworks, structure and directories, then writes a reviewable proposal to docs/_meta/discovery.proposed.yaml. Nothing is applied without your review.

pages.title: Quick Start
pages.intro: The full loop, end to end, in five commands.

blocks.code:
arah doctor
arah discover
arah domain sync
arah export-graph
arah evolve
blocks.label: THE LOOP

items: discover — propose domains from the repository

blocks.text: Each command writes versioned artifacts you review in pull requests. The harness never applies structural changes without human selection.

pages.title: Update
pages.intro: Keep the consumer consistent with the kernel through regeneration.

blocks.text: regenerate updates the kernel, re-runs discovery, syncs domains, regenerates graphs and validates the installation. Review the resulting diff like any other change.

pages.title: Uninstall
pages.intro: The harness is files in your repository — removal is a commit.

blocks.text: Delete the installed directories and config: .agents/, .skills/, .arah/, arah.config.yaml, AGENTS.md, the harness entries in scripts/ and .github/workflows/. Your code, docs and history are untouched.

blocks.code:
git rm -r .agents .skills .arah arah.config.yaml AGENTS.md
git commit -m "remove arah harness"
blocks.label: REMOVE

pages.title: Repository-first
pages.intro: The repository is the operational source of truth — not a platform, model or session.

blocks.text: Every contract the harness enforces is a versioned file: reviewable in PRs, diffable, portable across tools and models. There is no external database and no hidden state.

items: Context ownership: knowledge belongs to the repository
Independence: switch models without losing procedures
Auditability: history is git history plus the ledger

pages.title: Agents
pages.intro: An agent is a manifest before it is a model.

blocks.code:
# .agents/backend-payments.yaml
role: operational
domains: [payments]
paths: ["backend/payments/**"]
skills: [refactor, test-first]
autonomy: propose-only
escalation: payments-specialist
blocks.label: .agents/backend-payments.yaml

blocks.text: Manifests declare role (operational or advisor), scope (domains and paths), capabilities (skills) and autonomy. Gates validate every manifest against schemas/.

pages.title: Skills
pages.intro: Deterministic, executable procedures — not free-form prompts.

blocks.code:
# .skills/test-first/skill.yaml
name: test-first
inputs: [spec]
steps:
  - generate failing test
  - implement
  - run gates
outputs: [diff, test-report]
blocks.label: .skills/test-first/skill.yaml

blocks.text: A skill encodes "how we do things here" as a versioned unit an agent is explicitly authorized to run. Unauthorized skills are simply unavailable.

pages.title: Domains
pages.intro: Explicit areas of the repository with responsibilities, paths, references and validations.

blocks.code:
# docs/_meta/domains.yaml
payments:
  paths: ["backend/payments/**"]
  owners: [platform-team]
  advisors: [payments-specialist]
  gates: [spec, security]
blocks.label: docs/_meta/domains.yaml

blocks.text: Domains give agents the organizational map humans carry in their heads. Discovery proposes them; domain sync materializes what humans approve.

pages.title: Specialists
pages.intro: Consultative agents bound to a domain.

blocks.text: Specialists encode domain judgment: constraints, invariants, decision criteria. Operational agents consult them through the signal bus before changing domain-owned code. Their answers persist as typed events.

pages.title: Choreography
pages.intro: Path-based selection of agents, skills and gates.

blocks.code:
match: "backend/payments/**"
activate:
  agents: [backend-payments]
  advisors: [payments-specialist]
  gates: [spec, security, build]
blocks.label: harness/choreography.yaml

blocks.text: A change is routed by the files it touches. Out-of-scope agents are never activated — scope is structural, not behavioral.

pages.title: Autonomy
pages.intro: Declared authority levels per agent and per path.

blocks.code:
autonomy:
  default: propose-only
  overrides:
    - paths: ["docs/**"]
      level: execute-with-audit
blocks.label: arah.config.yaml

items: propose-only — agent produces a proposal; humans apply
execute-with-audit — agent applies within scope; ledger records everything
escalate — agent must consult before acting

pages.title: Human Gates
pages.intro: Merge authority is human, structurally.

blocks.text: No harness mechanism can merge. Evolution proposals, agent changes and code changes all terminate in a pull request reviewed by a human. This is a design invariant, not a configuration option.

pages.title: TechOrganism
pages.intro: The operating model: perceive, organize, signal, observe, propose, select, regenerate.

blocks.text: TechOrganism is the model by which a repository perceives its structure, organizes responsibilities, communicates signals, preserves memory and proposes its own evolution — under human selection. See the TechOrganism page for the full mapping.

pages.title: Discover
pages.intro: Observe the repository and propose domains and specialists.

items: Observes: stack, frameworks, structure, directories, domain hints

pages.title: Domain Sync
pages.intro: Materialize approved domain candidates into contracts.

blocks.text: Reads the approved discovery output and config, writes docs/_meta/domains.yaml and advisor manifests. Re-run after structural changes.

pages.title: Export Graph
pages.intro: Derive the agent graph from manifests.

blocks.text: Generates docs/_meta/graph.json — nodes for agents, skills and domains; edges for authorization and responsibility. A gate fails if the graph drifts from its sources.

pages.title: Signal Bus
pages.intro: Persistent, typed, append-only communication.

pages.title: Audit
pages.intro: The append-only ledger of actions, results and autonomy decisions.

blocks.text: Every session writes to .arah/audit/: actions taken, gates run, autonomy level in force, artifacts produced. The ledger is the evidence base for evolution proposals.

pages.title: Evolve
pages.intro: Propose improvements from accumulated evidence.

blocks.text: Analyzes ledger, signals and telemetry. Writes proposals to docs/_meta/evolution/ — e.g. splitting an overloaded domain. Proposals become PRs; humans select.

pages.title: Regenerate
pages.intro: Homeostasis for the installation.

items: Updates the kernel to the latest version

pages.title: Doctor
pages.intro: Diagnose the installation.

blocks.text: Checks kernel integrity, schema conformance, config validity and directory structure. Exit code 0 means healthy; non-zero codes identify the failing check.

pages.title: Sync Check
pages.intro: Run all gates locally, exactly as CI runs them.

blocks.code:
✓ manifests   ✓ specs
✓ docs        ✓ graph
✓ security    ✓ dependencies
✓ build
gates: 7/7 passed
blocks.label: OUTPUT

pages.title: Gates Overview
pages.intro: Executable validations that run locally and in CI. A failing gate blocks the pipeline.

items: Manifest validation — agents, skills, domains against schemas/
Spec gates — relevant changes require satisfied specs
Documentation gates — docs stay consistent with contracts
Security gates — secrets, dangerous patterns, scope violations
Dependency audits — known-vulnerable or unapproved packages
Build gates — the project must build and test
Agent graph validation — graph must match its sources

blocks.text: Where they run
The same scripts run in three places: on demand (arah sync-check), pre-commit (hooks) and in CI (.github/workflows/). A PR that fails validation cannot be merged.

pages.title: arah.config.yaml
pages.intro: The consumer-side policy surface.

blocks.code:
project: my-project
kernel: 0.4.1
autonomy:
  default: propose-only
profiles: [default]
overlays: []
gates:
  required: [manifests, build]
  per-domain: true
blocks.label: arah.config.yaml

blocks.text: Profiles select predefined policy sets; overlays layer client- or product-specific adjustments over the shared kernel without forking it.

pages.title: Schemas
pages.intro: Every contract validates against a schema.

blocks.text: schemas/ ships with the kernel and defines the shape of agent manifests, skill manifests, choreography rules, domain contracts and config. Schema validation is the first gate in every run.

pages.title: AI Environments
pages.intro: Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, Codex and custom agents — governed, not replaced.

blocks.text: AGENTS.md is the entry contract every environment reads. Installed .cursor/ rules bind Cursor; Claude Code and Copilot pick up AGENTS.md and the manifests directly. Custom agents integrate by reading the same files and writing to the same bus.
The guarantee
Because contracts live in the repository, switching environments loses nothing: the same roles, skills, gates and history apply to whichever tool you point at the repo.

pages.title: GitHub & CI
pages.intro: Gates in workflows; evidence in pull requests.

blocks.code:
# .github/workflows/arah-gates.yml
on: [pull_request]
jobs:
  gates:
    runs-on: windows-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: powershell -File scripts/arah-gates.ps1
blocks.label: .github/workflows/arah-gates.yml

blocks.text: The install step writes this workflow. Branch protection plus the gates job makes validation structurally unavoidable.

pages.title: CLI Reference
pages.intro: Select a command to see syntax, parameters, files read and generated.

pages.title: Signal Types
pages.intro: The typed vocabulary of the bus.

items: change.ready — a change prepared for PR

pages.title: Autonomy Levels
pages.intro: The three levels and what they permit.

items: propose-only — the agent produces proposals; humans apply them
execute-with-audit — the agent applies changes within scope; the ledger records every action
escalate — the agent must consult its advisor before acting

blocks.text: Levels are set per agent and overridable per path in arah.config.yaml. The default is propose-only.

pages.title: Exit Codes
pages.intro: CLI exit codes for scripting and CI.

pages.title: Generated Artifacts
pages.intro: Everything the harness writes, and where.

pages.title: Minimal Repository
pages.intro: The smallest useful installation.

blocks.text: One domain, one operational agent, default gates. Good for evaluating the loop end-to-end in an afternoon.

blocks.code:
arah install -ProjectName "minimal"
arah discover
arah domain sync
blocks.label: SETUP

pages.title: Monorepo
pages.intro: Territories with different rules.

blocks.text: Each product area becomes a domain with its own agents, advisors, gates and autonomy. Choreography keeps agents inside their territory; shared policies live once in arah.config.yaml.

pages.title: Regulated Environment
pages.intro: Evidence as a byproduct of the workflow.

blocks.text: Set autonomy to propose-only everywhere, require spec and security gates per domain, and enable full session telemetry. Every merge carries its evidence trail: specs, gate reports, consultation records, human approval.
